stem: Using periodicity and reference angles, evaluate \( \cos\!\left(\dfrac{[[k]]\pi}{6}\right) \).
solution: Subtract full periods and use the reference angle: \( \cos\!\left(\tfrac{[[k]]\pi}{6}\right)=[[answer]] \).

stem: Using the periodicity of tangent (period \( \pi \), not \( 2\pi \)) and a reference angle, evaluate \( \tan\!\left(\dfrac{[[k]]\pi}{4}\right) \).
solution: Since \( \tan \) has period \( \pi \) (half that of \( \sin \) and \( \cos \)), reduce \( \tfrac{[[k]]\pi}{4} \) modulo \( \pi \) to a reference angle: \( \tan\!\left(\tfrac{[[k]]\pi}{4}\right)=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This confuses the value with its reciprocal, \( \cot \).
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This confuses the tangent value with \( \sin \) of the same angle.
This confuses the tangent value with \( \cos \) of the same angle.

stem: Using periodicity and a reference angle, evaluate \( \sin([[deg]]^\circ) \).
solution: Subtract full \( 360^\circ \) rotations from \( [[deg]]^\circ \) and use the reference angle: \( \sin([[deg]]^\circ)=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This confuses the sine value with the cosine of the same reduced angle.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This subtracts the correct value from \( 1 \) instead of reporting it directly.
This confuses the sine value with the negative cosine of the same reduced angle.

stem: Using periodicity, a reference angle, and the fact that sine is an odd function, evaluate \( \sin\!\left(-\dfrac{[[k]]\pi}{6}\right) \).
solution: Since \( \sin \) is odd, \( \sin(-\theta) = -\sin(\theta) \). Reduce \( \tfrac{[[k]]\pi}{6} \) with periodicity and a reference angle first, then negate: \( \sin\!\left(-\tfrac{[[k]]\pi}{6}\right)=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets that sine is odd and drops the negative sign entirely.
This confuses the value with the cosine of the same positive angle.
This confuses the value with the negative cosine of the same positive angle (cosine is even, not odd).
This is off by one from the correct value.
This has the wrong sign and is off by one.
This incorrectly treats cosine as odd; \( \cos(-\theta)=\cos(\theta) \), not \( -\cos(\theta) \).
